sourceMovie.title: Code 8
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Code 8 (2019) as a science fiction theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The science fiction and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Samaritan
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Code 8 clicked for you, Samaritan (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Repo Men
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Code 8 clicked for you, Repo Men (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Code 8 clicked for you, Terminator 2: Judgment Day (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Code 8?
faq.answer: Try Samaritan, Repo Men, Terminator 2: Judgment Day—each shares DNA with Code 8 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Code 8?
faq.answer: Code 8 (2019) is best described as Science Fiction, Action, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Code 8 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Code 8 Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
